questions.question: What was the main aim of the East India Company when it first came to India?
questions.answer: The main aim of the East India Company was to trade and earn profits from Indian goods.

questions.question: Why was Bengal important to the East India Company?
questions.answer: Bengal was rich in resources and provided large revenue through agriculture and trade.

questions.question: Why did the Battle of Plassey become an important event in Indian history?
questions.answer: It allowed the British to gain control over Bengal and marked the beginning of British political power.

questions.question: How did Mir Jafar help the British in the Battle of Plassey?
questions.answer: Mir Jafar betrayed Siraj-ud-Daulah and supported the British during the battle.

questions.question: Why was the Battle of Buxar important for the British?
questions.answer: The victory strengthened British political power in India.

questions.question: What rights did the Company receive after the Battle of Buxar?
questions.answer: The Company received the Diwani rights to collect land revenue.

questions.question: Why was the Diwani important for the East India Company?
questions.answer: It allowed the Company to collect revenue and strengthen its financial power.

questions.question: What was the Subsidiary Alliance system?
questions.answer: It was a policy where Indian rulers accepted British troops and protection.

questions.question: Why did Indian rulers accept the Subsidiary Alliance?
questions.answer: They accepted it to receive British protection against enemies.

questions.question: What was the Doctrine of Lapse?
questions.answer: It was a policy allowing the British to annex states without a natural heir.

questions.question: Why did Lord Dalhousie introduce the Doctrine of Lapse?
questions.answer: He introduced it to expand British territories in India.

questions.question: Name one state annexed under the Doctrine of Lapse.
questions.answer: Jhansi was annexed under the Doctrine of Lapse.

questions.question: Why did the British fight wars with Mysore?
questions.answer: They wanted to control the region and reduce the power of Mysore rulers.

questions.question: Who were the rulers of Mysore who resisted the British?
questions.answer: Hyder Ali and Tipu Sultan resisted the British.

questions.question: Why was Tipu Sultan considered a strong opponent of the British?
questions.answer: He fought several wars against the British to protect his kingdom.

questions.question: Who were the Marathas?
questions.answer: The Marathas were a powerful Indian group that controlled large territories in western India.

questions.question: What were the Anglo-Maratha wars?
questions.answer: They were wars fought between the British and the Marathas for control of India.

questions.question: Why did the British want to control Indian territories?
questions.answer: They wanted political power and economic resources.

questions.question: How did political rivalry among Indian rulers help the British?
questions.answer: It weakened Indian rulers and made it easier for the British to gain control.

questions.question: Why did the East India Company maintain a strong army?
questions.answer: The Company maintained a strong army to protect its trade and expand territories.

questions.question: What role did diplomacy play in British expansion?
questions.answer: The British used alliances and treaties to increase their influence.

questions.question: Why did the British annex Awadh in 1856?
questions.answer: They claimed that its administration was inefficient.

questions.question: How did the Company change from traders to rulers?
questions.answer: The Company used wars, alliances and revenue control to gain power.

questions.question: Why were Indian rulers unable to stop British expansion easily?
questions.answer: They lacked unity and had internal conflicts.

questions.question: What was the long-term result of British expansion?
questions.answer: It led to the establishment of British colonial rule in India.
